title: Neolloydia gielsdorfiana
intro: Neolloydia gielsdorfiana, also known as the Dwarf Pincushion Cactus or Neolloydia, is a type of cactus plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality due to its calming and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of Neolloydia gielsdorfiana include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects. Neolloydia gielsdorfiana is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and other sleep disorders.
taxonomy: Neolloydia gielsdorfiana is a species of cactus in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is also known as Euphorbia gielsdorfiana. This plant is native to South Africa. Neolloydia gielsdorfiana belongs to the tribe Euphorbieae. It is a member of the subfamily Euphorbioideae within the family Euphorbiaceae.
